1.name: Pebble Stream Carp
1.description: A small carp that dwells in the shallows of the Yellow River. Its jade-green scales shimmer faintly in murky water.

2.name: River Marsh Sprite
2.description: A low spirit born from stagnant marshland. It moves without sound and leaves ripples shaped like a sleeping fish.

3.name: Tidal Lurker
3.description: A creature that follows the river's current, feeding on turbulence. It strikes swiftly before retreating into the deep.

4.name: Undertow Specter
4.description: A vengeful water wraith that seizes the strongest enemy and drags it below the surface, sending it back to the hand.

5.name: Coiling Mist Serpent
5.description: Hides beneath a veil of fog. When revealed, it coils around the mightiest foe and casts them back into the void.

6.name: Ripple Shade
6.description: Upon entering the field, it blurs reality for all who witness it, forcing one among them to vanish into shadow.

7.name: Deep Current Phantom
7.description: An entity of pure current that flows through all attacks undetected. No hand can grasp what does not hold form.

8.name: Midnight Tide Wraith
8.description: Born in the darkest hour before dawn. It smashes the field's greatest threat and buries another in shadow before vanishing upstream.

9.name: Veil Carp of Wujiang
9.description: Named for the fatal crossing at Wujiang. Its emergence casts one enemy aside and reveals the path forward for its summoner.

10.name: Sacred River Turtle
10.description: Revered across the Han dynasty as a messenger of heaven. Neither arrow nor blade can find purchase against its shell, and none may challenge it directly.

11.name: Yangtze Flood Caller
11.description: Channels the wrath of the Yangtze in flood season. Every monster on the opposing shore is swept back to the hand at once.

12.name: River Demon of Dongting
12.description: An ancient dark presence beneath Lake Dongting. It strips the opponent's hand before casting the strongest enemy back into oblivion.

13.name: Mirror Lake Sovereign
13.description: When revealed, its reflection projects across the entire field, sending all opposing forces back to the hand and shrouding one chosen creature in shadow.

14.name: Still Water Immortal
14.description: A being of perfect stillness that cannot be found by violence. Its arrival halts even the most determined assault in its tracks.

15.name: Abyssal Tide Empress
15.description: Her arrival scours both field and mind. All enemy forces return to the depths as the opponent is left completely empty-handed.

16.name: Eternal Current Sage
16.description: The river does not remember who opposes it. Untouchable and inexorable, it clears the field and reveals two truths to its summoner at once.

17.name: Floodgate Archivist
17.description: When the seal breaks, the flood erases all — monsters, enchantments, and traps alike. One creature is left face-down among the ruins.

18.name: Azure Dragon of the Eastern Sea
18.description: One of the four divine beasts. None may target it, none may strike it, and its mere presence washes all opposition from the field.

19.name: Bamboo Shoot Spirit
19.description: A tiny spirit that inhabits freshly sprouted bamboo. Slow to grow but stubborn as the roots it guards.

20.name: Mossy Stone Sapling
20.description: A small wood elemental covered in soft moss. It prefers defense over confrontation, letting the forest do the fighting.

21.name: Herbalist Vine Wisp
21.description: A healing spirit formed from medicinal herbs. Its arrival slowly restores the summoner's life force through the earth's own medicine.

22.name: Verdant Marsh Druid
22.description: A shapeless entity dwelling in wetland reed beds. Its presence draws healing energy directly from the soil beneath the field.

23.name: Bramble Thicket Shade
23.description: Entangles all surrounding forces in thorns the moment it arrives, sapping the strength and precision of every combatant present.

24.name: Sacrifice Blossom
24.description: A flower spirit that burns bright before fading. When cut down, its final dispersal bathes the field in restorative light.

25.name: Thorned Banyan Elder
25.description: A gnarled banyan that drains the vitality of all who stand against it, while drawing nourishment from the roots below to heal its summoner.

26.name: Forest Canopy Guardian
26.description: Its arrival invigorates all wood creatures on the field, channeling the energy of an ancient forest into every allied blade.

27.name: Wither Root Hermit
27.description: A withered old spirit that drains the strength of every combatant on the field. None escape its creeping influence.

28.name: Jade Canopy Sage
28.description: A great spirit of the forest who restores the summoner's vitality while breathing renewed power into every wood creature present.

29.name: Radiant Willow Martyr
29.description: It steps forward knowing it will fall. Its destruction becomes a gift — life restored in abundance and a new card drawn from the deck.

30.name: Strangling Banyan Sentinel
30.description: Coils its roots around every opponent and draws energy upward, fueling the summoner's health while the field weakens around it.

31.name: Ancient Cedar Patriarch
31.description: A towering spirit of immense age. Its presence invigorates every wood creature and heals the summoner through sheer ancestral force.

32.name: Illuminated Grove Arbiter
32.description: Brings light into the darkest thicket, weakening all combatants and revealing a hidden card to the summoner as the forest brightens.

33.name: Dual-Aspect Bodhi
33.description: A spirit that both nurtures and corrodes. It strengthens wood creatures while sapping the vitality of all who oppose the forest's will.

34.name: World Tree Emanation
34.description: A fragment of the mythic tree said to anchor heaven to earth. Wood creatures are elevated, enemies are diminished, and life flows freely.

35.name: Thousand-Year Strangler
35.description: A mass of roots and vines that has waited a millennium. Its grip saps every creature dry while flooding the summoner with recovered life.

36.name: Eternal Bloom Phoenix-Willow
36.description: Clad in radiant petals, it sacrifices itself willingly. Its fall heals generously, draws two cards, and leaves all wood creatures stronger in its memory.

37.name: Loess Pebble Sprite
37.description: A tiny creature made entirely of compressed yellow earth. Fragile to look at but nearly impossible to move.

38.name: Clay Bank Sentinel
38.description: A mass of riverbank clay given consciousness. It stands firm against all who approach, absorbing blow after blow.

39.name: Packed Earth Golem
39.description: Formed by ancient terraforming rites. Its body compresses further under each blow, growing harder with every strike it endures.

40.name: Mudwall Defender
40.description: Interposes itself between attacker and ally. Whenever a friendly monster is struck, it absorbs the full impact of the blow.

41.name: Rebound Earthen Shell
41.description: Its surface is so dense that force applied to it returns directly to the attacker. A walking paradox of offense and defense.

42.name: Buried Stone Ambusher
42.description: Hides face-down within the terrain. When revealed mid-attack, the stone erupts and shatters whatever creature dared to strike it.

43.name: Jade Barrier Colossus
43.description: A statue-like guardian that reflects all damage back to attackers while absorbing any harm directed at the summoner's life points.

44.name: Fortified Rampart Spirit
44.description: Each blow it endures reinforces it further. Damage that was meant to pass through becomes another layer of permanent fortified defense.

45.name: Spike Stonewall Ambusher
45.description: Covers itself in hidden stone spikes. Capable of piercing even the sturdiest defense, it waits face-down for any attacker bold enough to strike.

46.name: Great Granite Warden
46.description: Every attack that bounces off its body leaves a new layer of hardened stone behind. It grows effectively impenetrable over time.

47.name: Basalt Trap Guardian
47.description: Lies dormant until struck. With a roar it destroys the attacker and adds another stratum of basalt to its already immense frame.

48.name: Quake Warden of Taihang
48.description: Named for the great mountain range. Pierces through the strongest defense while shielding its summoner from all retaliatory battle damage.

49.name: Blood-Price Stone Titan
49.description: Demands a toll of life force before raising its full shield. Those who pay find all battle damage reflected and blocked simultaneously.

50.name: Immovable Jade Monolith
50.description: An ancient jade totem that cannot be destroyed by any force. It stands eternal, absorbing every attack ever directed at its summoner.

51.name: Ambush Terracotta Colossus
51.description: Modeled after the buried armies of a forgotten king. Its reveal shatters the attacker outright while its body hardens considerably from the impact.

52.name: Unbreaking Mountain Sovereign
52.description: The mountain does not yield. Indestructible, it drives through all defenses while deflecting every retaliatory force sent against it.

53.name: Eternal Wall of Heaven and Earth
53.description: A boundary between realms given form. Nothing can harm it, and any who dare strike it are crushed by the combined weight of the world.

54.name: Terraquake Emperor
54.description: The final expression of earth's dominance. Hidden until the moment is right, it pierces all defense, shatters its attacker, and hardens past all measure.

55.name: Copper Blade Footsoldier
55.description: The most basic unit of Han dynasty metal warfare. Armed with a simple bronze blade, it fights with more enthusiasm than skill.

56.name: Iron Spear Conscript
56.description: Drafted from the fields and handed a spear. More durable than he looks and willing to prove himself on any battlefield.

57.name: Tempered Vanguard
57.description: A front-line soldier whose iron armor has seen enough battles to bear its own scars. Reliable and hard to turn back.

58.name: Bronze Shield Warrior
58.description: A mid-rank soldier wielding a tower shield of hammered bronze. Reliable, balanced, and difficult to overcome in a straight fight.

59.name: Whetstone Duelist
59.description: Sharpens its blade the moment it steps onto the field. Its attack grows permanently as it prepares for every engagement ahead.

60.name: Layered Iron Sentry
60.description: Adds an extra plate of iron upon summoning. Balanced in offense and defense, it becomes harder to wound as the duel progresses.

61.name: Forged Steel Captain
61.description: A veteran officer whose arrival noticeably sharpens the entire force. His blade has never needed replacing.

62.name: Shadowsteel Berserker
62.description: A blade-wielder who fights without mercy or restraint. His strikes punch straight through armor, carving ruin into whatever lies behind the defense score.

63.name: Iron Cavalry Commander
63.description: A mounted officer whose arrival bolsters both offense and defense simultaneously. His scarred armor has never once failed him in the field.

64.name: Smelted War Colossus
64.description: Poured from a single mold of heated iron, this warrior grows more lethal with each step it takes onto the battlefield.

65.name: Twin-Blade Ironclad
65.description: Wields a paired set of blades forged from the same ingot. Balanced offense and growing defense make it a complete and dangerous combatant.

66.name: Nether Blade General
66.description: A general who has sworn himself to shadow. His strikes pierce all resistance, and his ferocity rises with every moment he remains on the field.

67.name: Grand Marshal of Steel
67.description: A decorated warlord whose presence instills fear. His attack permanently climbs while his armor turns aside whatever retaliatory force remains.

68.name: Molten Iron Overlord
68.description: Forged in a crucible of volcanic heat. Every blow is stronger than the last, and his capacity for violence knows no upper limit.

69.name: Obsidian Blade Sovereign
69.description: A dark lord of metal who drives his obsidian blade through whatever stands in his way. Even the gods of defense cannot stop his advance.

70.name: Heaven-Forged Warlord
70.description: Armored in metal said to have fallen from the sky. His attack and defense rise beyond mortal limits the moment he steps onto the field.

71.name: Great Iron Tyrant
71.description: A conqueror in full war armor who grows fearsome upon arrival and draws a new strategy in the same unbroken motion.

72.name: Darksteel Emperor
72.description: The apex of forged metal. Indomitable in dark power, his piercing blade cuts through all defenses while both attack and resilience climb without end.

73.name: Ember Wisp
73.description: A tiny flame spirit that drifts through battlefield smoke. Small but drawn irresistibly to the heat of conflict.

74.name: Cinder Hound
74.description: A dark creature of burning coal that spits sparks upon arrival, stinging the opponent before it has even settled onto the field.

75.name: Flame Viper
75.description: A serpent whose scales are living fire. It lunges forward upon summoning, scorching the opponent before retreating back into its coils.

76.name: Pyre Imp
76.description: A reckless spirit that hurls a fierce gout of flame at the opponent the moment it arrives. Its sole joy is in the burning.

77.name: Spite Flame Wraith
77.description: Even in death it burns. When cut down in battle, it releases all stored heat in a final cursed burst aimed directly at the opponent.

78.name: Balanced Pyre Monk
78.description: Channels fire with discipline. The flame it releases scorches the opponent, but the monk draws warmth from it to mend his own wounds.

79.name: Infernal Reaper
79.description: Steps onto the field burning everything at once. The weakest foe is destroyed directly as a wave of fire deals its full toll.

80.name: Defiant Blaze Champion
80.description: Refuses to go quietly. When felled in battle, the explosion of contained fire surges outward and scorches the enemy with its full stored fury.

81.name: Funeral Pyre Titan
81.description: Sacrifices itself to wipe the field clean. The resulting conflagration destroys every monster present and sends a scorching wave through the opponent.

82.name: Hellfire Stalker
82.description: It comes for the weakest and the rest feel the heat. Destroys the most vulnerable enemy and burns the opponent in the same relentless motion.

83.name: Controlled Flame Hermit
83.description: A disciplined fire sage who harnesses the flame with purpose, scorching the opponent while converting residual heat into healing energy.

84.name: Raging Pyre Berserker
84.description: In life it is already dangerous. In death it is catastrophic, dealing heavy burn damage and destroying the weakest remaining enemy monster.

85.name: Grand Conflagration Lord
85.description: A being of total fire who destroys every opposing monster on arrival and lets the residual heat scorch the opponent in kind.

86.name: Immolation Sovereign
86.description: Offers itself as fuel for a cataclysm. Every monster on the field is reduced to ash and a raging inferno tears through the opponent's life points.

87.name: Temperate Flame Arbiter
87.description: A rare fire entity that balances destruction with restoration. It burns, it judges, and it heals in the same measured, deliberate breath.

88.name: Inferno Tyrant of the South
88.description: One of the most feared fire entities. Its arrival scorches all enemies, burns the opponent directly, and grants the summoner a new card from the flames.

89.name: Total War Pyre Overlord
89.description: The price of its power is steep. Life force paid in advance buys total erasure of the field and absolute devastation aimed at the opponent.

90.name: Last Flame of Luoyang
90.description: Named for the burning of the ancient capital. Even after being destroyed, it takes every opposing monster with it and leaves ruin in its wake.

91.name: Minor Lung Hatchling
91.description: A fledgling dragon barely emerged from its shell. Even in infancy, its dark aura unsettles those who stand before it.

92.name: Scaled River Drake
92.description: A young dragon with a naturally piercing claw. Its strikes punch through even layered defense as if it were paper.

93.name: Iron-Jaw Wyvern
93.description: A stocky dragon variant with an exceptionally powerful bite. Its jaw strength exceeds that of most fully mature dragons.

94.name: Barbed Scale Dragon
94.description: Covered in backward-curved scales that tear through armor on contact. A single pass leaves an opponent's defense far worse for wear.

95.name: Brood Matriarch Drake
95.description: Her arrival galvanizes every dragon in the vicinity. Younger dragons on the field feel the surge of ancestral power coursing through their scales.

96.name: Undying Claw Serpent
96.description: A dragon that refuses to stay dead. When destroyed in battle, it reforms from the ashes and rises from the grave once more.

97.name: Shadow Wing Dragon
97.description: A dragon of pure darkness that bypasses all opposition, driving its claws directly at the summoner whenever no monsters stand in its way.

98.name: Dragon of Returning Bones
98.description: When it sets foot on the field, it calls another of its kin back from death, as if the grave itself bends to draconic will.

99.name: Dragon Vanguard Champion
99.description: A proud battle-dragon whose arrival unleashes dormant power in every dragon on the field. Under its banner, the strongest of its kin grow unstoppable.

100.name: Thunder Fang Dark Drake
100.description: A high-ranking dragon whose fangs crackle with dark energy. It bypasses defensive lines when clear and drives straight for the opposing duelist.

101.name: Sovereign Jade Wyrm
101.description: A dragon of divine jade that deflects all supernatural influence. No spell or trap can alter its course once it has been summoned.

102.name: Resurgent Blood Drake
102.description: Even when slain, it claws back from the grave. Its return fills every dragon on the field with renewed and terrible ferocity.

103.name: Dark Fang Warlord Dragon
103.description: A battle-hardened wyrm that leads dragon-kind into unstoppable assaults, piercing all defense and empowering its kin upon arrival.

104.name: Radiant Scale Behemoth
104.description: Clad in scales of luminous jade and immune to all sorcery. Its charge cuts through every layer of defense without slowing.

105.name: Sable Ancestor Dragon
105.description: A venerable dark wyrm who calls fallen kin back from the grave and invigorates all surviving dragons with ancestral power.

106.name: Void Wing Emperor
106.description: Immune to all effects, capable of striking directly when unopposed, and its claws drive through every defensive value without exception.

107.name: Inviolate Heaven Dragon
107.description: A divine dragon that cannot be destroyed, cannot be targeted by any effect, and whose piercing charge respects no barrier whatsoever.

108.name: Great Ancestor of Dragonkind
108.description: The progenitor of all dragons. Its arrival raises the fallen, emboldens every surviving kin, and reveals a new truth to the summoner.

109.name: Newborn Flame Chick
109.description: A phoenix barely hatched from its ember egg. Warm to the touch and flickering with the quiet promise of future rebirth.

110.name: Cinder Sparrow
110.description: A small phoenix that burns out quickly in battle. When it falls, it releases its stored warmth as a final healing gift.

111.name: Ashen Fledgling
111.description: A young phoenix still learning its nature. Its feathers are dull with ash but beginning to catch the light of its own future fire.

112.name: Hearthfire Aviator
112.description: A phoenix that has found its rhythm in the cycle. Each destruction releases a generous surge of healing energy.

113.name: Cyclical Ember
113.description: A phoenix attuned to the endless cycle of rebirth. When destroyed, it does not truly leave the field — it waits, and rises again.

114.name: Memory Ash Crane
114.description: A phoenix-crane of graceful temperament that combs the graveyard upon arrival, recovering one lost card to the summoner's hand.

115.name: Eternal Ember Guardian
115.description: A steadfast phoenix that carries the passive gift of revival. It can be destroyed but never permanently removed from the cycle.

116.name: Dusk Wing Reviver
116.description: A dark phoenix whose power lies not in its own survival but in dragging fallen allies back from death the moment it arrives.

117.name: Cycle Breaker Tailfeather
117.description: Gathers all dead cards back into the deck and draws from the renewed pool. The grave becomes the deck, and the cycle begins anew.

118.name: Radiant Pyre Sentinel
118.description: A brilliant phoenix that rises passively when destroyed and releases a surge of healing energy each time it is felled in battle.

119.name: Ashen Reaper Phoenix
119.description: A dark-aligned phoenix that strips a card from the opponent's hand while summoning a fallen ally back to the field in the same breath.

120.name: Purifying Flame Sage
120.description: Returns the dead to the deck, draws new cards, and restores life to the summoner. A phoenix that believes all things can be made whole again.

121.name: Undying Light Sovereign
121.description: A great phoenix whose revival is guaranteed and whose arrival simultaneously pulls one fallen ally back from death.

122.name: Rekindling Archive Phoenix
122.description: Reclaims all fallen cards back into the deck and draws generously from the restored pool in a single sweeping ritual.

123.name: Dark Cycle Predator
123.description: Brings allies back from death, strips a card from the opponent's hand, and salvages additional resources from the grave before settling.

124.name: Eternal Rebirth Phoenix
124.description: Returns fallen cards to the deck, draws new cards, revives a fallen ally, and cannot be put down permanently. It simply begins again.

125.name: Imperishable Celestial Fenghuang
125.description: Cannot be destroyed by any means. When it finally falls to battle, it restores substantial life to the summoner as its parting grace.

126.name: Void Harbinger Phoenix
126.description: Brings allies back from any grave, strips the opponent of cards, and reclaims lost resources from the graveyard before the ash clears.

127.name: Village Militia
127.description: A common soldier armed with what he could find. Not particularly skilled, but willing to fight for the cause without question.

128.name: Han Infantry Sergeant
128.description: A seasoned foot soldier who has survived enough campaigns to command a small unit of his own. Reliable under pressure.

129.name: Rallying War Cry Footman
129.description: Lets out a battle cry upon arrival that temporarily sharpens one ally's offense, carrying the momentum of a charge into the next attack.

130.name: Charging Pike Unit
130.description: Breaks into a full sprint the moment it reaches the field, temporarily boosting its own attack for a decisive and overwhelming charge.

131.name: Formation Keeper
131.description: A disciplined soldier who holds the line. Its arrival bolsters all warrior-class allies, steadying the formation around it.

132.name: Glory-Seeking Duelist
132.description: Every time this warrior deals battle damage, it draws a new card from the deck — feeding on the momentum of each successful strike.

133.name: Iron Brotherhood Captain
133.description: Raises warrior morale across the board and temporarily sharpens his own blade on arrival for the first decisive engagement.

134.name: Light of the Vanguard
134.description: A warrior who carries the standard of the light. His presence raises the fighting spirit of every warrior on the field.

135.name: Warrior King's Herald
135.description: Arrives with a younger warrior summoned from the hand, calling a comrade of limited strength into the fight for the greater cause.

136.name: Three Kingdoms Field Marshal
136.description: A general who emboldens all warrior allies and calls a capable comrade to his side from the hand upon arrival.

137.name: Righteous Blade General
137.description: A light-attribute warrior who inspires all warriors to fight at elevated levels and draws one card upon entering the field.

138.name: Battle Vein Commander
138.description: Every time this warrior deals battle damage, the energy of victory draws a new card and invigorates all warrior strength across the field.

139.name: Warlord of a Hundred Victories
139.description: A storied general whose arrival emboldens all warrior allies and calls a comrade of any strength from the hand to stand beside him.

140.name: Blessed Banner Commander
140.description: A warrior of righteous light whose arrival greatly swells the fighting strength of every warrior on the field at once.

141.name: Relentless Momentum General
141.description: Each time this warrior pushes through and deals damage, two cards are drawn and all warriors grow stronger in his wake.

142.name: Supreme Warlord of the Han
142.description: A legendary commander. Warrior morale surges, a new ally joins from the hand, and one card is drawn before battle truly begins.

143.name: Grand Marshal of Righteous Light
143.description: The pinnacle of the warrior lineage in light. Raises every warrior to fearsome levels and draws one card in the same breath.

144.name: Conqueror of Ten Thousand
144.description: Calls a new ally from the hand, emboldens all warriors considerably, and temporarily supercharges one warrior to devastating effect.

145.name: Zap Sparrow
145.description: A tiny creature of lightning that skips past all opposing monsters to strike the opponent directly. Small, fast, and utterly irritating.

146.name: Bolt Wisp
146.description: A formless arc of lightning that ignores all defensive lines and strikes the opposing summoner directly, turn after turn.

147.name: Thunder Sprite
147.description: A mischievous storm spirit that sends a stinging jolt of lightning into the opponent before it even settles onto the field.

148.name: Stormcloud Imp
148.description: Arrives crackling with built-up static. Unleashes a sharp burst of damage upon summoning before the opponent can react.

149.name: Resonance Striker
149.description: Delivers a disruptive lightning strike and rips a card from the opponent's hand in the same sudden, crackling arrival.

150.name: Heaven's Wrath Conduit
150.description: A neutral vessel for divine lightning that channels raw electrical force directly into the opponent the moment it lands.

151.name: Thunder Shock Tactician
151.description: Deals a sharp burst of lightning damage and draws one card upon arrival, using the clarity of the strike to read the battlefield ahead.

152.name: Static Mind Disruptor
152.description: Generates a burst of electromagnetic interference that strips a card from the opponent's hand and deals direct damage in the same motion.

153.name: Stormgate Arbiter
153.description: When it deals battle damage, it steals the opponent's next draw phase and rewards itself with an extra card from the deck in return.

154.name: Six Hundred Volt General
154.description: A high-ranking thunder entity that blasts a powerful jolt of damage and strips a card from the opponent's hand upon arrival.

155.name: Heaven Pillar Striker
155.description: Channels the full force of a heaven-sent bolt directly into the opponent in an overwhelming and unavoidable strike.

156.name: Enlightened Storm Sage
156.description: Calls on lightning for illumination as much as destruction. Draws two cards while dealing a precise jolt of shock damage at once.

157.name: Grand Thunder Strategist
157.description: Deals damage, disrupts the opponent's hand, and draws a card — all in the same decisive moment of arrival. Leaves nothing to chance.

158.name: Cycle Drain Thunderclap
158.description: Each time it deals battle damage, the opponent loses their next draw while the summoner gains a card and deals additional bonus damage.

159.name: Heavenfall Lightning Lord
159.description: Calls down a massive bolt of raw lightning and strips a card from the opponent's hand as the charge strikes the field.

160.name: Supreme Storm Sovereign
160.description: Arrives with the force of a full tempest. Draws two cards, deals devastating storm damage, and strips the opponent's hand nearly bare.

161.name: Void Sky Thunder Emperor
161.description: Deals punishing thunder damage, disrupts the opponent's hand, and denies them their next draw phase in a single unrelenting entrance.

162.name: Celestial Judgment Bolt
162.description: The final word in thunder. An overwhelming divine judgment crashes down on the opponent as the storm clears and one card is drawn.

163.name: Dread Claw Fusion Wyrm
163.description: A dragon fused from shadow and scale, immune to all effects and relentless in its piercing assault against any defense it encounters.

164.name: Imperishable Shadow Dragon
164.description: The pinnacle of dark dragon fusion. Piercing, effect-immune, and indestructible — it cannot be stopped by any conventional means.

165.name: Dragon-Warrior Tide Caller
165.description: Born of both dragon blood and warrior spirit. Its arrival supercharges every dragon and warrior on the field with ancestral ferocity.

166.name: Supreme Dragon-Warrior Sovereign
166.description: Commands both dragon-kind and warrior-kind simultaneously, enraging each to their limits and drawing one card to prepare the final push.

167.name: Ironclad War Fusion
167.description: A warrior fused to devastating effect. One monster is enraged to terrifying levels while all warriors on the field are emboldened.

168.name: Cleaving War-Fusion General
168.description: Pierces through all defense while permanently enraging one ally to fearsome levels and raising every warrior on the field to match.

169.name: Cycle Flame Fusion Bird
169.description: A phoenix fused with eternal fire. It rises again when destroyed and deals a punishing burst of damage each time it falls.

170.name: Undying Flame Fusion Sovereign
170.description: The ultimate phoenix fusion. Revives permanently, deals catastrophic damage on each death, and destroys the weakest enemy as a farewell gift.

171.name: Storm Call Fusion Entity
171.description: Arrives like a sudden thunderclap, drawing two cards and hurling every opposing monster back to the hand in one sweeping motion.

172.name: Tempest Sovereign Fusion
172.description: Draws two cards, sends all enemy monsters back to the hand, and deals additional damage in the same devastating entrance.

173.name: Tidal Wrath Fusion
173.description: A water fusion that sweeps all opposing monsters from the field while draining the strength of every remaining creature.

174.name: Flood Sovereign Fusion
174.description: Clears the field, weakens all remaining creatures, and heals the summoner substantially in a single sweeping arrival.

175.name: Eternal Stone Fusion
175.description: Indestructible and impassive, it reflects all battle damage directly back at every attacker while standing completely immune to destruction.

176.name: Terraquake Fusion Colossus
176.description: Cannot be destroyed, pierces all defenses, and sends all battle damage back to the source. The pinnacle of earth and stone combined.

177.name: Annihilation Drake Fusion
177.description: Destroys every opposing monster on arrival and fans a scorching wave of damage across the field as a finishing touch.

178.name: Extinction Fang Fusion
178.description: Pierces all defense, destroys every opposing monster, and burns the opponent for good measure in the same merciless entrance.

179.name: Eternal Cycle Phoenix Fusion
179.description: Pierces defense, revives fallen allies, and cannot be permanently destroyed. The cycle carries it forward regardless of the opposition.

180.name: Sovereign Rebirth Phoenix Fusion
180.description: Piercing, effect-immune, self-reviving, and capable of raising fallen allies upon arrival. The cycle in its most complete and perfect form.

181.name: Strategic Retreat Formation
181.description: A basic war formation that forces the enemy's strongest unit to withdraw from the battlefield and return to the hand.

182.name: Flood the Field
182.description: A sweeping tactical maneuver that sends every opposing force retreating from the field in complete disarray.

183.name: Field Physician's Tonic
183.description: An ancient herbal remedy administered by a battlefield physician. Restores a meaningful measure of life without delay.

184.name: Weakening Incense Ritual
184.description: A debilitating smoke fills the field, sapping all combatants while the summoner recovers vitality in the same cloud.

185.name: Jade Wall Construction
185.description: Reinforces one ally's defense considerably using layers of jade-brick fortification built in moments.

186.name: Iron Tortoise Formation
186.description: Covers one ally in layered iron, significantly fortifying its defense and absorbing the next wave of battle damage completely.

187.name: Whetstone Blessing
187.description: Sharpens one ally's blade before battle. The target is considerably enraged, its attack rising to meet the challenge ahead.

188.name: Battle Hardening Ritual
188.description: A full forge ritual that both enrages and fortifies a monster simultaneously, sharpening its blade and thickening its armor.

189.name: Fire Arrow Volley
189.description: A barrage of flaming arrows deals a burst of fire damage to the opponent and reduces the weakest opposing monster to ash.

190.name: Red Cliff Conflagration
190.description: Calls upon the infamous inferno of Red Cliff. Every opposing monster is destroyed and the residual flames burn the opponent as well.

191.name: Resurrection Bell
191.description: The temple bell rings once. A fallen ally returns from the graveyard to stand on the field.

192.name: Dragon Revival Rite
192.description: Raises a fallen ally and simultaneously emboldens every dragon on the field with renewed fighting strength.

193.name: Cycle of Rebirth
193.description: Returns all cards in the graveyard to the deck and draws new ones from the reshuffled pool.

194.name: Unified Grave Rite
194.description: Raises one fallen ally from either graveyard and returns all other dead cards to the deck in the same sacred ceremony.

195.name: Warrior's Battle Hymn
195.description: A rousing war hymn that considerably raises the fighting strength of every warrior currently on the field.

196.name: War Drum Summons
196.description: The battle drum calls — all warriors are emboldened and one new warrior arrives from the hand to answer.

197.name: Thunder Arrow Dispatch
197.description: An electrified projectile delivers a powerful lightning strike to the opponent and rips a card from their hand.

198.name: Disruption Formation
198.description: Deals a disruptive burst of damage, strips the opponent's hand nearly bare, and denies them their next draw phase entirely.

199.name: Scholar's Foresight
199.description: A strategist consults the stars and draws two cards. Zhuge Liang himself could not refuse such clarity before a battle.

200.name: Burning Strategy Scroll
200.description: Draws three new strategies at the cost of discarding one — trading a known plan for three unknown possibilities.

201.name: Assassination Order
201.description: A sealed imperial decree delivered at midnight. The strongest threat on the field is eliminated without ceremony.

202.name: Talent Scouting Scroll
202.description: A strategist combs through military records to find the right warrior for the moment. Searches the deck for a capable ally.

203.name: Torch the Supply Lines
203.description: A swift cavalry raid burns every enemy formation and magical support structure to the ground.

204.name: Arrow Volley
204.description: A concentrated barrage of arrows deals a sharp burst of direct damage to the opposing summoner.

205.name: Defection Edict
205.description: An irresistible imperial decree that permanently converts one opposing monster to the summoner's side.

206.name: Honeytrap Stratagem
206.description: A temporary ruse that turns one enemy monster against its own summoner, dealing damage in the confusion before it is released.

207.name: Sow Confusion in the Ranks
207.description: Disrupts the opponent's preparations, forcing the top cards of their deck into the grave before they can be used.

208.name: The Burning of Luoyang
208.description: Dong Zhuo's infamous scorched-earth order. Every monster on the field is destroyed without distinction.

209.name: Call to Arms
209.description: A war horn echoes across the field. One warrior answers from the hand, stepping onto the field without cost.

210.name: Blood Pact with Heaven
210.description: Life force paid in blood. In exchange, the summoner may search any card they desire from the depths of the deck.

211.name: Iron Shield Formation
211.description: When the enemy charges, soldiers lock shields and absorb the full impact. No damage reaches the summoner.

212.name: Pit Trap of the Eastern Pass
212.description: A concealed pit dug across the line of attack. The attacker falls in and does not return.

213.name: Bronze Mirror Ward
213.description: A polished shield returns all battle force directly to the one who sent it.

214.name: Fog of the River Valley
214.description: A sudden veil of mist rolls in as the charge begins, causing the assault to lose its way entirely.

215.name: Ambush at the Gorge
215.description: The moment a new threat steps onto the field, it walks directly into a waiting ambush that destroys the strongest among them.

216.name: Feigned Retreat
216.description: As the enemy arrives, forces pull back in practiced disorder, drawing the strongest newcomer off the field in the confusion.

217.name: Chaos in the Rear Guard
217.description: As the enemy launches their assault, a flanking maneuver disrupts their command structure and forces a card from their hand.

218.name: Double Feint
218.description: Absorbs the assault entirely and uses the momentum to hurl the field's strongest threat back to the hand.

219.name: Tempered Counter-Stance
219.description: Reflects all damage back at the attacker and permanently hardens the defending monster from the experience.

220.name: Exploding Siege Engine
220.description: A booby-trapped war machine destroys the attacker and sends a burst of damage through the opposing ranks.

221.name: Spy in the Camp
221.description: The moment the opponent summons a new monster, an embedded spy disrupts their strategy and strips one card from their hand.

222.name: Calm Before the Storm
222.description: Halts the incoming assault entirely and grants the summoner a moment of clarity to draw a new card in the sudden quiet.

223.name: Spring Trap at the Gate
223.description: As the enemy's strongest monster arrives, a concealed trap springs and deals additional damage in the chaos of its destruction.

224.name: Impenetrable Gorge Pass
224.description: The attacker is swallowed whole by the terrain — destroyed utterly — and the damage it carried never reaches the summoner.

225.name: Flood the Valley
225.description: At the moment of the enemy's arrival, floodgates open and the entire opposing force washes back to the hand.

226.name: Officer's Bronze Sword
226.description: A standard-issue weapon given to field officers of the Han. Sharpens the wielder's attack considerably.

227.name: Lacquered War Shield
227.description: A hardened lacquer shield carried by veteran soldiers. Considerably fortifies the bearer against incoming blows.

228.name: Iron-Forged Cavalry Blade
228.description: A longer blade designed for mounted combat. Enrages the wielder and sharpens their attack to a fearsome edge.

229.name: Layered Scale Armor
229.description: Multiple layers of iron scales overlapping like dragon skin. Significantly fortifies the bearer against any blow.

230.name: Tempered General's Halberd
230.description: A weapon reserved for generals who have earned it in blood. The wielder's attack rises to commanding levels.

231.name: Jade-Inlaid Warplate
231.description: Armor set with jade fragments said to hold divine protection. The bearer becomes exceptionally hard to harm.

232.name: War-Horse Saddle and Spear
232.description: A matched set of cavalry equipment. Enrages the wielder's attack while bolstering their defense in equal measure.

233.name: Blade of the Five Generals
233.description: A legendary weapon said to have been carried by each of the Five Tiger Generals in turn. Its bearer strikes with devastating force.

234.name: Dragon-Bone Warsuit
234.description: Armor and weapon forged from the bones of a fallen dragon. Both attack and defense rise to terrifying levels.

235.name: Chituma's Riding Harness
235.description: The legendary war harness of Chituma, Lu Bu's immortal red stallion. The bearer is transformed into an unstoppable force.

236.name: Bamboo Seed Token
236.description: A single stalk of bamboo planted on the battlefield. It holds no power of its own, but in the right hands becomes something more.

237.name: Dragon Egg
237.description: An unhatched dragon egg crackling with latent dark energy. Something stirs inside, waiting for the right moment.

238.name: Dying Ember
238.description: A last ember clinging to existence. More heat than monster, it fades quickly but refuses to go out entirely.

239.name: Puddle Spirit
239.description: A tiny water spirit barely large enough to fill a cup. It cannot fight, but is harder to remove than it appears.

240.name: Wounded Soldier
240.description: A foot soldier who has already taken one too many hits. Barely standing but unwilling to leave the field.

241.name: Yellow River Sovereign
241.description: An ancient water deity of immense power who strips the opponent's hand and banishes their mightiest warrior in one sweeping motion.

242.name: Flood Dragon of the Han
242.description: A primordial entity that cannot be targeted by any power. Its arrival destroys every opposing force without exception.

243.name: Abyss Leviathan of the Eastern Deep
243.description: A creature of myth that arrives like the end of an era. Strips the opponent bare, destroys all that stands, and draws two cards from the ruins.

244.name: Tide Warlord of the Wei River
244.description: Commands the tidal forces of the Wei River to sweep all opposing monsters from the field while disrupting the enemy's hand.

245.name: Eternal Current Tyrant
245.description: A force of nature that neither strategy nor force can stop. All opposing monsters are wiped away and the opponent is denied their next move.

246.name: River Wrath Colossus
246.description: A colossal water entity that hurls the enemy's strongest fighter from the field and scorches the opponent in the same unstoppable surge.

247.name: Midnight Tide Sovereign
247.description: Rises from the darkest depths to sweep all opposition from the field and drain every remaining creature of its strength.

248.name: Azure Heaven Flood Dragon
248.description: A divine dragon of light and water that cannot be found by any attack. Its arrival clears the field and reveals one card to the summoner.

249.name: Calamity Surge Overlord
249.description: Strips the opponent's hand, banishes their strongest warrior, and scars the opponent directly in one coordinated flood assault.

250.name: Celestial Inundation Sovereign
250.description: The greatest of the water lords. Clears the field, strips the opponent's hand twice over, and draws two cards in the stillness that follows.

251.name: Twin-Current War Serpent
251.description: Destroys the strongest opposing monster and hurls the next strongest back to the hand in the same two-pronged assault.

252.name: Black River Chancellor
252.description: An entity of dark water authority that strips a card, sweeps the entire field, and denies the opponent their next draw in one calculated entrance.

253.name: Torrent Purifier Sovereign
253.description: Destroys every opposing spell and trap before sweeping all remaining monsters from the field in an unstoppable cleansing flood.

254.name: Radiant Tide Enforcer
254.description: A being of luminous water that scorches the opponent, banishes their greatest threat, and strips a card from their hand in one arrival.

255.name: Untouchable Deep Sovereign
255.description: Cannot be targeted or attacked by any means. From perfect safety it strips the opponent's hand nearly bare without lifting a claw.

256.name: Ancient Forest Sovereign
256.description: A primordial forest entity that saps all combatants of their strength while restoring generous life to the summoner.

257.name: Imperishable Verdant Titan
257.description: Cannot be destroyed by any force. Its mere presence on the field restores the summoner's life to an overwhelming degree.

258.name: World Grove Emanation
258.description: A being of supreme natural power. It heals generously, weakens all enemies, and invigorates every wood creature on the field at once.

259.name: Entangling Root Colossus
259.description: A mass of ancient roots that saps all combatants of their fighting edge while drawing life upward for the summoner.

260.name: Great Canopy Patriarch
260.description: Rises above the battlefield like a great tree, invigorating wood creatures, draining enemies, and restoring the summoner's vitality all at once.

261.name: Thousand Root Colossus
261.description: A vast root network that saps all opposing strength while restoring the summoner's life through nourishment drawn from the earth.

262.name: Withering Dark Canopy
262.description: A great dark canopy that starves all combatants of light and strength, leaving the summoner revitalized in the growing gloom.

263.name: Radiant Forest Sovereign
263.description: A forest entity of pure light that restores life, weakens all opposition, and draws one card as clarity blooms through the canopy.

264.name: Martyr of the Sacred Grove
264.description: Falls willingly for the cause. Its destruction floods the summoner with life and reveals one new card from the deep roots of the deck.

265.name: Eternal Verdant Sovereign
265.description: The pinnacle of wood power. Heals the summoner dramatically, emboldens all wood creatures, and saps every enemy on the field.

266.name: Creeping Vine Colossus
266.description: A vast tangle of aggressive vines that drains every combatant of strength while restoring the summoner's life in the process.

267.name: Living Forest Oracle
267.description: A great wood spirit that emboldens all wood creatures, heals the summoner, and draws one card as the forest whispers its secrets.

268.name: Indestructible Dark Canopy
268.description: Cannot be destroyed by any force. It saps all combatants of their fighting edge while restoring the summoner's life through the darkness.

269.name: Luminous Sacrifice Tree
269.description: A tree of pure light that makes the ultimate sacrifice. Its destruction floods the field with healing and weakens all remaining combatants.

270.name: Heaven Root Patriarch
270.description: A root that reaches all the way to heaven. Restores life generously, emboldens all wood creatures, and denies the opponent their next draw.

271.name: Imperishable Jade Fortress
271.description: Cannot be destroyed by any means. All battle damage aimed at it flows directly back at the attacker without exception.

272.name: Heaven-Wall Earth Colossus
272.description: Indestructible and absolute. Absorbs all damage aimed at allied monsters and destroys every attacker in the same motion.

273.name: Eternal Dark Mountain
273.description: Cannot be destroyed, cannot be targeted, and returns all battle damage without mercy. A wall that has stood since before time.

274.name: Granite Ambush Colossus
274.description: Lies face-down like a slab of rock. When struck, it erupts to destroy the attacker and absorbs every point of incoming damage.

275.name: Quake Sovereign of the Nine Peaks
275.description: Pierces through all defense while permanently fortifying allied monsters and absorbing all retaliatory battle damage.

276.name: Basalt Counterguard
276.description: Absorbs every blow aimed at allied monsters and converts the impact into a burst of damage against the opposing summoner.

277.name: Dark Ironwall Titan
277.description: Reflects all battle damage back at every attacker and permanently fortifies the defending monster from the experience.

278.name: Radiant Trap-Stone Colossus
278.description: Reveals itself at the perfect moment, destroys the attacker, reflects all damage, and hardens permanently from the confrontation.

279.name: Eternal Stone Strategist
279.description: Cannot be destroyed. Each time it deals battle damage, the summoner draws a card — an indestructible wall that also thinks.

280.name: World-Ending Earth Sovereign
280.description: Indestructible and piercing. Reflects all damage and destroys every attacker in the same ruthless, unceasing motion.

281.name: Stalwart Jade Wall
281.description: When any allied monster is attacked, the attacker is destroyed and all resulting damage is absorbed without trace.

282.name: Double-Mirror Stone Titan
282.description: Its reflective surface is doubled in potency. Damage returned to the attacker is amplified, and a burst of energy also strikes the opponent directly.

283.name: Shadow Fortress Sovereign
283.description: Cannot be targeted. It shields allied monsters, destroys every attacker, and receives not a single point of damage in return.

284.name: Sacred Stone Revealer
284.description: Waits face-down until the moment is right. Its reveal destroys the attacker and dismantles every opposing spell and trap at once.

285.name: Timeless Granite Sovereign
285.description: Cannot be destroyed and reflects all damage. When it acts, the opponent is denied their next draw phase entirely.

286.name: Iron Conqueror of the North Plains
286.description: A piercing metal warrior of supreme caliber. Its arrival enrages its own attack to devastating levels.

287.name: Heaven-Forged Battle Sovereign
287.description: A warrior of mythic metal who enrages its own attack and fortifies its defense beyond all mortal limits, piercing every resistance.

288.name: Darksteel Conqueror Supreme
288.description: The pinnacle of dark metal. Pierces, strikes directly when unopposed, enrages its own attack to terrifying levels, and draws two cards from sheer momentum.

289.name: Iron Legion Commander
289.description: A powerful metal warrior that enrages its own attack and fortifies its defense the moment it steps onto the field.

290.name: Radiant Steel Warlord
290.description: A warrior of piercing light whose arrival enrages its own attack and emboldens every metal creature on the field.

291.name: Piercing Iron Champion
291.description: A seasoned metal warrior who sharpens its own attack upon arrival and drives through any defensive value it encounters.

292.name: Shadow Steel Overlord
292.description: A dark metal commander who arrives fully enraged, sharpening attack and hardening defense simultaneously.

293.name: Iron Storm General
293.description: Pierces all resistance, enrages its own attack to terrifying levels, and scorches the opponent directly upon arrival.

294.name: War-Iron Field Marshal
294.description: A great metal general whose arrival enrages its own attack and emboldens every metal warrior on the field.

295.name: Celestial Blade Sovereign
295.description: A being of divine metal that pierces all defense, strikes directly when unopposed, and enrages its own attack beyond all measure.

296.name: Plated Iron Stalwart
296.description: A piercing metal warrior who fortifies its own defense upon arrival, combining aggressive advance with growing resilience.

297.name: Bronze War Sovereign
297.description: A warrior whose blade and armor are both sharpened and hardened upon arrival, growing more complete with each passing moment.

298.name: Black Iron Annihilator
298.description: A dark metal destroyer that pierces all resistance, enrages its own attack, and eliminates the weakest remaining enemy upon arrival.

299.name: Radiant Blade Conqueror
299.description: A metal warrior of radiant light that enrages its own attack and bypasses defensive lines to strike the opposing summoner directly.

300.name: Iron Heaven Sovereign
300.description: A supreme metal warrior of piercing force. Enrages its own attack beyond reckoning and draws one card from the clarity of total dominance.

301.name: Pyre Sovereign of the South
301.description: Burns the opponent with a scorching burst and destroys every opposing monster in the same blazing arrival.

302.name: Obliteration Flame Overlord
302.description: Demands a toll of life force before unleashing total annihilation. Every monster on the field is consumed and the opponent is scorched to the core.

303.name: Grand Conflagration Sovereign
303.description: A supreme fire entity of divine light. Destroys all opposing forces, scorches the opponent directly, and draws two cards from the smoldering aftermath.

304.name: Ruthless Pyre Commander
304.description: Eliminates the weakest enemy, scorches the opponent, and draws one card — a cold and calculated display of fire's precision.

305.name: Deathfire Colossus
305.description: A fire titan of immense grudge. When finally felled, it takes every opposing monster with it and scorches the opponent from beyond destruction.

306.name: Firestorm Raider
306.description: Scorches the opponent and eliminates the weakest enemy in the same reckless, two-pronged assault.

307.name: Balanced Pyre Sovereign
307.description: A rare fire entity that scorches the opponent while drawing restorative warmth back to the summoner in the same measured breath.

308.name: Sacrifice Inferno Overlord
308.description: Offers itself to the pyre. Its self-immolation destroys every monster on the field, scorches the opponent, and draws two cards from the ash.

309.name: Radiant Blaze Sovereign
309.description: A fire lord of pure light whose arrival reduces every opposing monster to cinders and fans the residual flames at the opponent.

310.name: Eternal Funeral Pyre
310.description: Even in destruction it acts. When sent to the grave by any means, it scorches the opponent and takes every opposing monster with it.

311.name: Radiant Pyre Champion
311.description: A light-aligned fire warrior who scorches the opponent and eliminates the weakest enemy in the same decisive engagement.

312.name: Spite Fire Warlord
312.description: Refuses to go quietly. When destroyed in battle, it detonates with enough force to scorch the opponent and draw one final card.

313.name: Tempest Flame Sovereign
313.description: Scorches the opponent, strips a card from their hand, and eliminates the weakest remaining enemy in a single overwhelming arrival.

314.name: Suppression Blaze Commander
314.description: Burns the opponent and denies them their next draw — a tactician who uses fire as a tool of total strategic control.

315.name: Blood Price Inferno Overlord
315.description: Offers life force in exchange for total annihilation. Every monster on the field is destroyed and the opponent bears the full weight of the blast.

316.name: Obsidian Terror Dragon
316.description: Strikes directly when unopposed and pierces all defense otherwise. Its arrival scorches the opponent as it descends from the darkness.

317.name: Void Fang Sovereign Dragon
317.description: Pierces, strikes directly, deflects all effects, and scorches the opponent upon arrival. Nothing in the field can challenge its presence.

318.name: Divine Light Conquest Dragon
318.description: The supreme dragon of light. Destroys all opposition, scorches the opponent, and cannot be touched by any spell or effect.

319.name: Dragon Herald of Unification
319.description: Pierces all defense, emboldens every dragon on the field, and draws one card as its clarion call echoes across the battlefield.

320.name: Dark Warlord Dragon of the North
320.description: Pierces, strikes directly when unopposed, emboldens every dragon greatly, and calls a fallen kin back from the grave in one sweeping entrance.

321.name: Iron Claw War Drake
321.description: A dark dragon that pierces all defense and scorches the opponent directly upon arrival.

322.name: Blood Fang Dragon Commander
322.description: A piercing dragon who scorches the opponent and emboldens every dragon on the field in the same entrance.

323.name: Sanctified Blade Dragon
323.description: Immune to all effects and piercing in every attack. Its arrival delivers a scorching blow to the opponent that no counter can deflect.

324.name: Dragon of Unbroken Advance
324.description: Pierces all resistance and strikes the summoner directly when clear. Scorches on arrival and draws one card from the momentum.

325.name: Shadow Titan Dragon Supreme
325.description: The apex of dark dragonkind. Piercing, direct-striking, effect-immune — scorches the opponent and draws two cards upon a devastating arrival.

326.name: Drake Vanguard of the Advance
326.description: Arrives with a comrade from the hand, scorches the opponent, and pierces all defense from the very front of every charge.

327.name: Dragon Ancestor of the Second Calling
327.description: Strikes directly when unopposed, calls a fallen dragon back from the grave, and emboldens every remaining kin on the field.

328.name: Nether Drake Revenant
328.description: Pierces all defense, deflects all effects, raises a fallen dragon from the grave, and scorches the opponent as it settles.

329.name: Radiant Dragon of the Vanguard
329.description: A piercing dragon of light who emboldens all kin and eliminates the weakest remaining enemy in the same entrance.

330.name: Dragon Sovereign of Ten Thousand Battles
330.description: Pierces, strikes directly, scorches the opponent, and calls a new ally from the hand — a warlord that arrives already winning.

331.name: Eternal Dawn Phoenix
331.description: A great phoenix that rises again when destroyed and calls one fallen ally back from either grave upon arrival.

332.name: Sovereign Flame of Undying Light
332.description: Cannot be destroyed and will rise again regardless. Its arrival raises an ally from the grave and restores the summoner's life generously.

333.name: Imperishable World Phoenix
333.description: Indestructible, self-reviving — it raises an ally, returns all dead cards to the deck, and draws two cards in the same sacred entrance.

334.name: Cycle Flame Sovereign
334.description: A phoenix that carries revival in both directions. It rises again when destroyed and drags a fallen ally back from the grave when struck down.

335.name: Dark Cycle Phoenix
335.description: A dark phoenix that returns all dead cards to the deck and draws new ones, reshaping the summoner's resources in the same entrance.

336.name: Selfless Pyre Bird
336.description: Rises again when destroyed and releases restorative energy each time it falls — a phoenix that gives as much as it takes.

337.name: Remembrance Phoenix
337.description: A phoenix that recalls both itself and others. Its arrival raises an ally and draws one card while the cycle of rebirth continues.

338.name: Vengeful Pyre Sovereign
338.description: A phoenix that answers its own death with wrath — dealing a devastating blow to the opponent, reviving itself, and raising a fallen ally in the chaos.

339.name: Dark Archive Phoenix
339.description: A dark phoenix that combs the graveyard for a recovered card and draws one more from the deck — a harvester of what death leaves behind.

340.name: Light Sovereign of the Eternal Cycle
340.description: Indestructible and self-reviving. Its arrival raises an ally, heals the summoner, and scorches the opponent — a phoenix that gives and takes in equal measure.

341.name: Ember Seer Bird
341.description: A phoenix of quiet wisdom. When destroyed, it leaves one final insight drawn from the deck in its place.

342.name: Consuming Flame Phoenix
342.description: Rises again when destroyed, raises a fallen ally, and strips a card from the opponent's hand in the same breath.

343.name: Undying Archive Phoenix
343.description: Cannot be touched by effects and rises of its own accord. Raises an ally and returns all dead cards to the deck in one sacred motion.

344.name: Dark Martyr Phoenix
344.description: A phoenix that embraces destruction. Each time it falls, the summoner is healed and one card is drawn — death repaid in full.

345.name: Cycle Sovereign Phoenix
345.description: Rises again, raises an ally, scorches the opponent, and draws two cards. A phoenix that treats the end of every cycle as a new beginning.

346.name: Grand Army Commander
346.description: Emboldens every warrior on the field and calls two new allies from the hand in a single commanding arrival.

347.name: Legendary Warlord of the Alliance
347.description: Emboldens all warriors greatly, calls two allies from the hand, and draws one card — a general who arrives already victorious.

348.name: Heaven's Chosen Warrior King
348.description: Emboldens all warriors to their absolute limit, calls two new allies to the field, and temporarily supercharges one warrior to staggering levels.

349.name: Rallying General of the Three Kingdoms
349.description: Calls two allies from the hand while emboldens every warrior on the field in the same rallying cry.

350.name: Shadow Army Marshal
350.description: A dark warlord who strikes directly when unopposed, emboldens all warriors greatly, and calls a new ally from the hand in one unstoppable entrance.

351.name: Rising General's Vanguard
351.description: Emboldens all warriors and calls one new ally from the hand — the first wave of a much larger campaign.

352.name: Dark War Surge Commander
352.description: Emboldens all warriors and temporarily supercharges one ally's attack in the same fierce arrival.

353.name: Iron Tide Warlord
353.description: Emboldens every warrior, calls two allies to the field, and scorches the opponent in the chaos of the combined arrival.

354.name: Radiant Battle Sovereign
354.description: Calls two warriors from the hand and draws two cards in the same breath — every arrival is also a strategic revelation.

355.name: Eternal War God of the Han
355.description: The pinnacle of warrior power. Emboldens all warriors to their limits, calls two allies, and draws three cards in a single overwhelming entrance.

356.name: Advancing Iron Captain
356.description: Emboldens all warriors and temporarily sharpens one ally's attack upon arrival, readying the field for the charge ahead.

357.name: War Banner Bearer
357.description: Emboldens all warriors, calls a new ally from the hand, and draws one card — the standard-bearer who also fights.

358.name: Shadow Conqueror's Host
358.description: A piercing dark warrior who emboldens every warrior greatly and calls a new ally from the hand in the same entrance.

359.name: Righteous Army Sovereign
359.description: A general of pure light who emboldens all warriors and calls two new allies from the hand in a single moment of inspiration.

360.name: World Conqueror's Host
360.description: Emboldens every warrior enormously, calls two allies to join the battle, and scorches the opponent as the army arrives in full force.

361.name: Grand Thunder Sovereign
361.description: Delivers a devastating jolt to the opponent and strips two cards from their hand in the same crackling arrival.

362.name: Heaven's Judgment Thunder Lord
362.description: Scorches the opponent, strips two cards from their hand, and denies them their next draw phase — a complete act of divine punishment.

363.name: Cataclysm Storm Sovereign
363.description: The ultimate thunder entity. Strips the opponent's hand nearly bare, deals catastrophic lightning damage, and draws two cards from the chaos.

364.name: Void Storm Arbiter
364.description: Strips two cards from the opponent's hand and delivers a sharp burst of storm damage in the same calculated thunderclap.

365.name: Enlightened Thunder Tyrant
365.description: Scorches the opponent, denies their next draw, and draws one new card for itself — lightning that both punishes and illuminates.

366.name: Storm Vanguard Striker
366.description: An elite thunder warrior who scorches the opponent and disrupts their hand in the opening move of every engagement.

367.name: Dark Sky Disruption Lord
367.description: Scorches the opponent, strips a card from their hand, and denies their next draw — a storm that closes off every avenue of recovery.

368.name: Absolute Thunder Sovereign
368.description: Scorches the opponent, strips two cards from their hand, and forces the top of their deck into the grave — leaving nothing intact.

369.name: Suppression Thunder Commander
369.description: Scorches the opponent, eliminates their weakest monster, and denies their next draw in a single act of total storm dominance.

370.name: Heaven's Wrath Absolute
370.description: The final expression of thunder's power. Strips the opponent's hand bare, deals overwhelming damage, and denies their next move entirely.

371.name: Lightning Herald
371.description: A fast-moving thunder entity that scorches the opponent and disrupts their planning with one sharp, well-timed arrival.

372.name: Thunder Sage of the Storm Eye
372.description: Strips two cards from the opponent's hand, deals a burst of storm damage, and draws one card from the clarity within the eye of the storm.

373.name: Dark Archive Thunderclap
373.description: Scorches the opponent, denies their next draw, and recovers a lost card from the grave — a storm that takes from the future and restores the past.

374.name: Restorative Thunder Sovereign
374.description: Delivers a lightning strike, strips two cards from the opponent's hand, and channels the leftover energy into healing the summoner.

375.name: Purification Storm Sovereign
375.description: Scorches the opponent, strips their hand, and dismantles every opposing spell and trap in a single sweeping storm of judgment.

376.name: Dragon Annihilator Fusion
376.description: A fused dragon of relentless piercing force whose arrival destroys every opposing monster without exception.

377.name: Dragon Strike Fusion Commander
377.description: Pierces and strikes directly, scorching the opponent upon its fused arrival.

378.name: Iron Fusion Sovereign
378.description: A warrior of fused iron and shadow who pierces all defense, enrages its own attack, and emboldens every warrior on the field.

379.name: Warlord Union Fusion
379.description: A fused warrior sovereign who emboldens all warriors, calls a new ally from the hand, and draws one card in a single moment of unity.

380.name: Blaze Cycle Fusion Bird
380.description: A fused phoenix that rises again when destroyed and punishes the opponent with a burst of damage each time it falls.

381.name: Eternal Pyre Fusion Sovereign
381.description: Rises again from any destruction, deals catastrophic damage when felled, and destroys the weakest remaining enemy as a departing gift.

382.name: Storm Tide Fusion Entity
382.description: A fused thunder entity that scorches the opponent and hurls every opposing monster back to the hand in the same arrival.

383.name: Heaven Hammer Fusion
383.description: Scorches the opponent, clears the field of all monsters in a single sweep, and strips a card from the opponent's hand in the aftermath.

384.name: Flood Tide Fusion Sovereign
384.description: Strips the opponent's hand of two cards and sweeps every opposing monster from the field in one combined flood assault.

385.name: Great Deluge Fusion Sovereign
385.description: Clears the entire field and drains the strength of every remaining creature in the aftermath of the flood.

386.name: Jade Fortress Fusion
386.description: Indestructible and immovable, it reflects all battle damage directly back at every attacker without fail.

387.name: World-Wall Fusion Colossus
387.description: Indestructible and piercing, it drives through all defenses and returns all damage to those foolish enough to strike it.

388.name: Dread Fusion Dragon of Annihilation
388.description: A fused dark dragon whose arrival destroys every opposing monster and fans a burst of fire through the opponent.

389.name: Supreme Annihilation Dragon Fusion
389.description: Pierces all defense, destroys every opposing monster, and scorches the opponent in the same annihilating entrance.

390.name: Radiant Cycle Phoenix Fusion
390.description: Pierces defense, revives fallen allies, and rises again when destroyed. The cycle in radiant, fused form.

391.name: Sovereign Eternal Phoenix Fusion
391.description: Piercing, effect-immune, self-reviving, and capable of raising fallen allies. The most complete expression of the cycle in fused form.

392.name: Absolute Dragon Fusion Sovereign
392.description: The supreme dragon fusion. Indestructible, immune to all effects, and piercing — its arrival destroys every opposing monster without exception.

393.name: Undying Heaven Phoenix Fusion
393.description: The supreme phoenix fusion. Indestructible, self-reviving, piercing — it raises fallen allies and scorches the opponent from an unassailable position.

394.name: Lu Bu's Dragon of End Times
394.description: The most feared fusion in existence. Pierces, strikes directly, deflects all effects, destroys all opposition, and scorches the opponent upon descent.

395.name: Empty the Granaries
395.description: A devastating strategy that strips three cards from the opponent's hand, severing their resources at the root.

396.name: Purge the Fallen
396.description: Removes every card in the opponent's graveyard from play entirely and draws two cards from the clarity that follows.

397.name: Battle of the Red Cliffs
397.description: Channels the decisive strategy of Red Cliff. Every opposing monster is destroyed and the opponent bears the brunt of the aftermath.

398.name: Siege of Luoyang
398.description: Strips two cards from the opponent's hand and denies them their next draw — a total strategic blockade.

399.name: Imperial Restoration Rite
399.description: A grand ceremony performed only in times of dire need. Restores a substantial measure of life to the summoner.

400.name: Withering Banquet Poison
400.description: A toxin slipped into the feast. Every combatant on the field is sapped of strength while the summoner's life is quietly restored.

401.name: Heaven's Price
401.description: Life force offered to the heavens. Every monster on the field is swept away and three cards are drawn from the cleared horizon.

402.name: General's War Decree
402.description: An official military decree that pushes one monster's attack to fearsome new heights permanently.

403.name: Rite of the Iron General
403.description: A sacred war ceremony that enrages a monster's attack and fortifies its defense simultaneously.

404.name: Heaven's Mandate Armament
404.description: A divine arming that pushes one monster's attack beyond all mortal limits and draws two cards as the heavens acknowledge the mandate.

405.name: March of the Iron Legion
405.description: The drums sound and every warrior on the field strides forward with greatly renewed fighting spirit.

406.name: War God's Summons
406.description: Greatly emboldens all warriors on the field and calls a new comrade from the hand to answer the divine call to battle.

407.name: Grand Army Rising
407.description: Elevates every warrior on the field enormously and calls two new allies from the hand in a single rallying moment.

408.name: Phoenix Bell Ritual
408.description: A solemn bell rings twice — once for the fallen ally called back from either grave, once for the card drawn in their honor.

409.name: Cycle of the Eternal Return
409.description: Returns every dead card to the deck and draws three new ones. The graveyard empties; the deck awakens.

410.name: Ceremony of the Undying Flame
410.description: The most sacred revival rite. Raises a fallen ally, reclaims all dead cards to the deck, and draws three new cards in the same breath.

411.name: Ballista Volley
411.description: A massed ballista strike delivers a concentrated burst of damage directly to the opposing summoner.

412.name: Catapult Bombardment
412.description: A devastating siege barrage scorches the opponent and disrupts their hand in the chaos of the impact.

413.name: Heaven's Thunder Artillery
413.description: An overwhelming lightning strike scorches the opponent and denies them their next draw phase entirely.

414.name: Defection of the Honored General
414.description: A legendary general switches sides. One opposing monster is permanently converted to the summoner's cause.

415.name: Imperial Blood Pact of Loyalty
415.description: Life paid in blood to secure the greatest possible defection. One enemy is permanently turned and two cards are drawn from the transaction.

416.name: Three Strategies of Huang Shigong
416.description: The legendary three scrolls of strategy, said to contain the sum of all military wisdom. Three cards are drawn in a single reading.

417.name: Great Scholar's Gambit
417.description: Trade one known strategy for four unknown ones. A risk only a true strategist would take.

418.name: Enlightenment Under Fire
418.description: Draws four cards while dealing a burst of damage to the opponent — insight and aggression in one seamless motion.

419.name: Conscription Orders
419.description: Searches the military rolls and drafts two capable warriors directly into the summoner's hand.

420.name: Blood Price Scouting
420.description: Life force paid for intelligence. Two cards of any kind are pulled directly from the deck into the hand.

421.name: Grand Strategy Compilation
421.description: Three specific cards are sought from the deck and one additional card is drawn — the most thorough tactical preparation possible.

422.name: Burn the Strategy Tents
422.description: A night raid that destroys every opposing spell and trap before they can be activated.

423.name: Scorched Earth Raid
423.description: Every opposing spell and trap is burned to the ground, and the weakest remaining monster is cut down in the same sweep.

424.name: Rot the Supply Lines
424.description: Forces the top of the opponent's deck into the grave, cutting them off from future resources.

425.name: Siege Exhaustion
425.description: A prolonged siege forces a large portion of the opponent's deck into the grave and strips a card from their hand.

426.name: Total Attrition Strategy
426.description: A crushing volume of the opponent's deck is forced into the grave and their next draw is denied — a war of absolute exhaustion.

427.name: Infiltrate the Archives
427.description: Spies infiltrate the opponent's strategic archives, sending cards from the top of their deck into the grave.

428.name: Destroy the War Records
428.description: A large portion of opposing cards are forced into the grave and one card is drawn from the summoner's own clarity of purpose.

429.name: Battle Fury Ritual
429.description: A powerful but temporary rite that pushes one monster into a battle rage, dramatically sharpening its attack for the coming engagement.

430.name: Heaven's Wrath Bestowed
430.description: One monster is seized by divine fury, its attack sharpened to overwhelming levels, while one card is drawn from the momentum of wrath.

431.name: Iron Siege Formation
431.description: Absorbs the next wave of battle damage entirely and permanently fortifies one monster from the experience.

432.name: Mirror Shield of the North Gate
432.description: Reflects all incoming damage back at the attacker and permanently hardens one defending monster from the impact.

433.name: Final Offering of the Warlord
433.description: A general who gives everything. The tributed card becomes the ignition for total annihilation and a devastating final blow to the opponent.

434.name: The Fall of the Han Dynasty
434.description: The card that ends eras. Every monster on the field is destroyed, the opponent is overwhelmed with crushing damage, and three cards are drawn from the ashes of an empire.

435.name: Siege Gate Ambush
435.description: The moment the enemy's strongest warrior steps through the gate, the trap springs and denies them their next move entirely.

436.name: Exploding Rampart
436.description: A section of the fortress wall rigged to detonate. The attacker is destroyed and the blast scorches the opposing summoner.

437.name: Polished Bronze Parry
437.description: The attack is turned back on the attacker with perfect precision, and the summoner draws one card from the clarity of the counter.

438.name: Flood Gate Ambush
438.description: As the enemy arrives, every opposing monster is swept from the field and one card is stripped from the opponent's hand.

439.name: Three-Stage Counter Maneuver
439.description: The attack is negated, the strongest threat hurled back to the hand, and one card is drawn from the resulting strategic clarity.

440.name: Spike Pit Formation
440.description: Absorbs all damage, destroys the attacker, and lets the residual force burst outward at the opposing summoner.

441.name: Annihilation Ambush
441.description: The entire opposing force walks into a perfectly laid trap. Every monster is destroyed and the opponent is denied their next draw.

442.name: Mist Screen Maneuver
442.description: The attack dissipates into sudden fog and the summoner draws one card in the quiet that follows.

443.name: Retreat and Strike
443.description: The attack is halted, the strongest threat hurled back to the hand, and a burst of damage finds the opponent in the chaos.

444.name: Grand Counter-Formation
444.description: The attack is not merely stopped — the attacker is destroyed in the attempt and two cards are drawn from the decisive counter.

445.name: Burning the Letter of Alliance
445.description: A spy intercepts and destroys two key communications the moment the enemy reinforces, leaving their strategy in disarray.

446.name: Total Intelligence Blackout
446.description: Every incoming communication is intercepted. Three cards are stripped from the opponent's hand and their next move is denied entirely.

447.name: Double Mirror Counter
447.description: A layered reflective formation. The force of the attack is doubled and sent directly back at the attacker.

448.name: Triple Mirror Riposte
448.description: The attack is caught in three successive mirrors and returned with three times its original force.

449.name: Heaven's Mirror Formation
449.description: The attack is reflected back with overwhelming amplified force and a residual burst of energy also scorches the opponent directly.

450.name: Counter-Stratagem
450.description: The moment the opponent activates a card effect, it is negated and the offending card is destroyed in the same motion.

451.name: Total Countermeasure
451.description: When the opponent attempts to use any card effect, it is nullified and every remaining spell and trap they control is dismantled at once.

452.name: Heaven's Edict of Nullification
452.description: The ultimate counter. Any card effect is negated, every opposing monster is destroyed, and two cards are drawn from the resulting silence.

453.name: Spy in the Draw Pile
453.description: Each time the opponent draws a card, an informant strips one from their hand — a persistent and grinding disruption.

454.name: Endless Harassment Formation
454.description: When the opponent draws, two cards are immediately stripped from their hand and their next draw is denied as well.

455.name: Iron Fortress Counter
